content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA

EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1100 K Street
Sacramento 95814
(916) 322-5660

October 25, 1976

Jack L. Boyd
Assistant County Superintendent
Merced County Department of Education
632 West 13th Street
Merced, California 95340

Dear Mr. Boyd:

     I have been asked to respond to your letter of September 10, 1976, which raises the following question:

     Since the Regional Migrant Program covers five counties and is but a fraction of the County Schools operation, will it be necessary for this office to submit the "Conflict of Interest Code" adopted by the County Board of Education to the Fair Political Practices Commission or should it be submitted to our County Board of Supervisors.

     It is our conclusion that, notwithstanding the fact that the Merced County Department of Education has been designated as the Local Education Agency (LEA) to administer the Migrant Education Program in your region by the State Department of Education, your county department, basically, is a local government agency with jurisdiction wholly within your county. Accordingly, the provisions of Government Code Section 82011(b) are controlling and the County Board of Supervisors is your Code reviewing body.

     Pursuant to the provisions of Public Law 93-380, and California Education Code Sections 551 through 553, the State Board of Education has divided California into 9 regions and designated a local education agency (LEA) in each region as the fiscal agent of the state to administer the federally funded Migrant Education Program. The fact that your local government agency has been designated as the fiscal agent of the state for this limited purpose does not, for purposes of the Political Reform Act, change your county department into a local government agency with jurisdiction in more than one county.

     If I may be of further assistance to you in this matter, please let me know.

                                                   Sincerely,

                                                   Edgar A. Kerry, Staff Attorney
                                                   Conflicts of Interest Division

EAK:mra

MERCED COUNTY DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION
WILLIAM H. STOCKARD, EdD. SUPERINTENDENT
632 WEST 13th STREET
MERCED, CALIFORNIA 95340
(209) 723-2031

F P P C

SEP 13 9 17 AM '76

September 10, 1976


FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California
95804

Gentlemen:

This administration has a question on the "Conflict of
Interest Code" that needs answering. The situation is
as follows:

The Office of Merced County Superintendent of Schools is
the Local Educational Agency for the Regional Migrant
Education Program. This program covers five (5) counties
which include Madera, Merced, Stanislaus, San Joaquin,
and Contra Costa Counties. The Office of Merced County
Superintendent of Schools is responsible for the employment, instruction, and fiscal aspects of the program.

QUESTION: Since the Regional Migrant Program covers five
counties and is but a fraction of the County Schools
operation, will it be necessary for this office to submit
the "Conflict of Interest Code" adopted by the County
Board of Education to the Fair Political Practices Commission
or should it be submitted to our County Board of Supervisors.

Please advise.

Sincerely,

JACK L. BOYD
Assistant County Superintendent,
Business Administration

JLB:jp


Educational Service and Leadership

sections.question:
Since the Regional Migrant Program covers five 
counties and is but a fraction of the County Schools 
operation, will it be necessary for this office to submit 
the "Conflict of Interest Code" adopted by the County 
Board of Education to the Fair Political Practices Commission 
or should it be submitted to our County Board of Supervisors. 
Please advise. 
JLB: jp
sections.conclusion: It is our conclusion that, pursuant to the provisions of Public Law 93-380, the Merced County Office of Education has been designated by the State Board of Education as the local educational agency (LEA) to administer the program in your region. As a local agency within the meaning of the Political Reform Act (Government Code Section 82111 et seq.), the Office of the County Superintendent of Schools is subject to the code reviewing procedures of the County Board of Supervisors.
sections.question_synthetic: Should the Merced County Office of the Superintendent of Schools, which administers a regional migrant education program covering five counties, submit its Conflict of Interest Code to the FPPC or to the County Board of Supervisors?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Merced County Office of Education, as the designated local educational agency (LEA), is subject to code review procedures under the County Board of Supervisors rather than needing separate FPPC submission.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document contains OCR artifacts and formatting issues that impacted clarity of some text. The question and conclusion sections were reconstructed from partially damaged source material. The second page contains the incoming request dated September 10, 1976, and the first page contains the FPPC response dated October 25, 1976.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Since the Regional Migrant Program covers five 
counties and is but a fraction of the County Schools 
operation, will it be necessary for this office to submit 
the "Conflict of Interest Code" adopted by the County 
Board of Education to the Fair Political Practices Commission 
or should it be submitted to our County Board of Supervisors. 
Please advise. 
JLB: jp

CONCLUSION: It is our conclusion that, pursuant to the provisions of Public Law 93-380, the Merced County Office of Education has been designated by the State Board of Education as the local educational agency (LEA) to administer the program in your region. As a local agency within the meaning of the Political Reform Act (Government Code Section 82111 et seq.), the Office of the County Superintendent of Schools is subject to the code reviewing procedures of the County Board of Supervisors.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that the Merced County Superintendent of Schools, designated as the Local Educational Agency for a five-county Regional Migrant Education Program, should submit its Conflict of Interest Code to the County Board of Supervisors for review rather than directly to the FPPC. The determination is based on the office's status as a local agency under the Political Reform Act.
